verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is secreted as a latent complex in which the LAP homodimer non-covalently (but tightly) wraps the mature dimer, and the whole complex is covalently linked via disulfide bonds to latent TGF-beta binding proteins (LTBPs) that in turn are covalently cross-linked to ECM or to cell-surface TM proteins (e.g. GARP/LRRC32, NRROS) on regulatory T cells and platelets. The GARP-TGFB1 surface complex is a documented wash-resistant, disulfide-tethered display on the outer face of Tregs and platelets — this is not a transient receptor-binding equilibrium but a stable covalent tether via GARP's TM scaffold. This constitutes 'stable_surface_attachment' meeting the wash-resistance criterion. The surface display is restricted to specific cell types (Tregs, activated platelets) and cell-activation states, making it contextual rather than constitutive. Antibody programs (e.g. anti-GARP:TGFb1 bispecific antibodies) explicitly target this cell-surface-tethered form, confirming therapeutic relevance of the surface pool.
